iterations.strategy: Root cause (Category A + B): The iter_0 inner loop has two critical flaws that compound. First, it uses long double for sum_disc_price and sum_charge, forcing x87 FPU execution — this disables all SSE/AVX2 SIMD and runs at 1 op/instruction vs 4 with AVX2. Second, every row in every block incurs a shipdate read and a branch (if sd[i] > CUTOFF), even though 597 of 600 blocks are ALL_PASS (zone map max_val <= 10471) and need no per-row date check at all. The fix has two parts: (1) extend zone map classification to distinguish ALL_PASS / MIXED / SKIP blocks; for ALL_PASS blocks (99.8% of work), use a branchless inner loop that does not load l_shipdate, enabling the compiler to auto-vectorize with AVX2; (2) replace all long double accumulators with double, eliminating x87 and enabling 4-wide SIMD throughout. Additionally, madvise(SEQUENTIAL|WILLNEED|HUGEPAGE) is applied to all mmap'd columns to maximize hardware prefetch and eliminate TLB pressure from 4KB pages, and OpenMP schedule is changed from dynamic(4) to static for lower synchronization overhead.

Root cause (Category A + B): iter_0's inner loop uses long double for sum_disc_price and sum_charge, forcing x87 FPU execution. X87 serializes computation into ~40-cycle dependency chains (1.0L-ld -> lp*omd -> lp*omd*(1+tax)), blocking the CPU's reorder buffer from seeing future memory loads and suppressing hardware prefetch. This limits effective DRAM bandwidth to ~31 GB/s (observed: 2.28 GB / 73ms) versus the hardware's 150+ GB/s capability. Fix (Category B): replace all long double accumulators with double throughout — eliminates x87, enables SSE2/AVX2 for FP operations, shortens dependency chains to ~4-8 cycles, and allows many more concurrent DRAM prefetch requests. Fix (Category A): extend zone map reading to produce a three-way block classification (ALL_PASS/MIXED/SKIP). For ALL_PASS blocks (597 of 600), skip the l_shipdate column load entirely — saves 597*100000*4 = 238.8 MB of DRAM reads and removes the per-row branch, giving the compiler a straight-line inner loop to optimize. For MIXED blocks (~3 of 600), load l_shipdate and apply the per-row predicate as before. Critically excluded: no madvise(WILLNEED/HUGEPAGE) calls — these caused iter_1's regression from 81ms to 112ms by triggering kernel THP remapping and prefetch overhead on already page-cached hot data. Schedule stays dynamic(4) as in iter_0.

Root cause (Category E + B): Two compounding inefficiencies. First, l_quantity, l_discount, l_tax, and l_extendedprice are stored as double (8 bytes each) despite having integer semantics — qty is 1-50, discount is 0.00-0.10 in 0.01 steps, tax is 0.00-0.08 in 0.01 steps. This wastes 3.7× DRAM bandwidth (2.04GB scan vs 0.54GB with compact encodings). Second, the Accum struct uses long double for sum_disc_price, forcing x87 FPU in the inner loop and preventing SIMD. Fix (Category E): Build uint8_t compact versions of qty/discount/tax and int32_t centiprice for extendedprice — all rows, built in 0.8s. Fix (Category B): Replace all floating-point accumulators with pure int64_t using integer-scaled arithmetic: sum_disc_price_i += price_i32 × (100 - disc_u8), sum_charge_i += price_i32 × (100 - disc_u8) × (100 + tax_u8). Integer arithmetic eliminates x87 entirely, shortens dependency chains to 3-5 cycles, and allows the compiler to use integer SIMD. Long double is kept only for the final 6-row output conversion (sum_disc_price_i × 0.0001L, sum_charge_i × 1e-6L), preserving %.4f/%.6f output precision without affecting the hot path. Expected improvement: main_scan from 34ms to ~5-9ms from 3.7× bandwidth reduction and elimination of x87 from the inner loop.
